name: HD 75426

name: HIP 91479
archival_rv_summary: Gaia DR3: 30 RV transits, rv_amplitude_robust=3.90 km/s, rv_chisq_pvalue=6.75e-5 (highly variable, peak-to-peak matches predicted K1~1.95 km/s for joint astro+spec orbit fit)
notes: AstroSpectroSB1 joint astro+spec fit. P=855.84d, e=0.815. a_phot=2.33mas -> a_1=0.127AU. From Kepler with M_1~0.7Msun: M_2=64 MJ (independent of inclination). K_1 from Gaia spec Thiele-Innes (C,H) = 574 m/s; K_1 from Gaia rv_amplitude_robust/2 = 1948 m/s — 3.4x INCONSISTENT, suggests either unmodeled second body, activity jitter, or normalization issue in Pourbaix C,H formula. Astrometric M_2 measurement is robust; spectroscopic mass interpretation more uncertain. HGCA Brandt 2024 chi^2=50.3 (independent 25-yr PM anomaly). LP 335-104 high-PM K-dwarf at 55.6 pc.

name: HIP 60321
archival_rv_summary: AstroSpectroSB1 fit: 30 RV transits, rv_amplitude_robust=6.54 km/s, P=530 d, e=0.34. K_1 from spec TI=106 m/s vs K_1 from rv_amp_robust/2=3272 m/s (30x discrepancy — methodology caveat as for HIP 91479). M_2 from astrometric Kepler is robust at 54 M_J.
filters_passed: 34/35 (failed only the inappropriate RUWE<5 cut for AstroSpectroSB1) + AstroSpectroSB1 joint + HGCA Brandt 2024 chi^2=42.17
notes: Originally filtered out by tier_a RUWE<5 cut (RUWE=5.77, but RUWE>1.4 is expected for AstroSpectroSB1 with real companion). HIGHEST significance AstroSpectroSB1 in our pool (92.9). a_phot=1.85 mas -> a_1=0.093 AU. Kepler M_2=54 MJ for assumed M_1=0.55 Msun (K-dwarf). HGCA chi^2=42.17 (strong 25-yr PM anomaly INDEPENDENT of NSS fit). K_1 inconsistency 30x (see CHECK_REPORT.md methodology caveat). Was missed by original pipeline due to RUWE filter applied uniformly across solution types.
